sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id Int32, event_date Date32, description String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;

INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date, description) VALUES (1, fromDaysSinceYearZero32(738200), 'Test Event 1'), (2, fromDaysSinceYearZero32(1), 'Test Event 2'), (3, fromDaysSinceYearZero32(0), 'Test Event 3');
SELECT id, event_date, description FROM test_dates WHERE event_date < fromDaysSinceYearZero32(3);
SELECT id, event_date, description FROM test_dates WHERE event_date = (SELECT fromDaysSinceYearZero32(1) FROM test_dates WHERE id = 2);
SELECT id, event_date, description, event_date -> id AS expr FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, count(*) FROM test_dates GROUP BY id HAVING count(*) > (SELECT count(*) FROM test_dates WHERE event_date IS NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE test_aggregations (id Int32, start_date Date32, end_date Date32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO test_aggregations (id, start_date, end_date) VALUES (1, fromDaysSinceYearZero32(5), fromDaysSinceYearZero32(10)), (2, fromDaysSinceYearZero32(15), fromDaysSinceYearZero32(20));
SELECT id, max(start_date) AS max_start FROM test_aggregations GROUP BY id HAVING max_start > fromDaysSinceYearZero32(10);
SELECT id, fromDaysSinceYearZero32(max(start_date) + (x -> x))(1) AS days_since FROM test_aggregations GROUP BY id;
